title: Order Certified Death Certificates
description: Order 10–15 certified copies of the death certificate. You will need these for almost every institution you contact. The funeral home can usually order them, or you can request them from your state's vital records office.
deadline_rule: Within first week — needed for all other tasks
contact_phone: Contact your funeral home or state vital records office

title: Obtain EIN for the Estate
description: Apply for an Employer Identification Number (EIN) for the estate using IRS Form SS-4. This is needed to open an estate bank account and file the estate tax return. Apply online at IRS.gov — takes about 15 minutes.
deadline_rule: Early in process — needed to open estate account

title: Retain a Probate Attorney
description: Consult with a probate attorney in the deceased's state of residence. Even if the estate is simple, an attorney can help you avoid costly mistakes. Many offer free initial consultations.

title: Report Death to Social Security Administration
description: Notify SSA of the death as soon as possible. The funeral home often does this automatically — confirm with them. Any Social Security payment received for the month of death must be returned. Do NOT spend it.
deadline_rule: As soon as possible — overpayments must be returned

title: Apply for SSA Survivor Benefits
description: If the deceased had a spouse, children under 18, or dependent parents, they may be eligible for survivor benefits. Call SSA or visit your local office to apply. Benefits are not automatic — you must apply.
deadline_rule: Apply promptly — benefits are not retroactive beyond 6 months

title: Cancel Medicare Coverage
description: Medicare coverage ends on the date of death. Notify Medicare to stop any automatic payments and prevent future billing issues. If the deceased had Medicare Advantage or Part D, notify those plans separately.

title: Notify VA of Death
description: If the deceased was a veteran, notify the VA immediately. VA benefits stop at death and overpayments must be returned. The VA also provides burial benefits including burial in a national cemetery, a burial flag, and a Presidential Memorial Certificate.
deadline_rule: As soon as possible — overpayments must be returned

title: Apply for VA Survivor Benefits (DIC)
description: Dependency and Indemnity Compensation (DIC) is available to surviving spouses, children, and parents of veterans who died from service-related causes. Apply using VA Form 21P-534EZ.
deadline_rule: Apply within 1 year of death for retroactive benefits

title: Cancel U.S. Passport
description: Mail the deceased's passport to the U.S. Department of State with a cover letter requesting cancellation. They will return it canceled with a hole punched through it. This prevents identity theft.
deadline_rule: Within a few months — prevents identity theft

title: Remove from Voter Rolls
description: Contact your county elections office to remove the deceased from voter registration records. This prevents potential voter fraud and is required by law in many states.

title: Notify DMV / Cancel Driver's License
description: Notify your state's DMV of the death to cancel the driver's license and prevent identity theft. If the deceased had vehicle registrations, these will need to be transferred or canceled separately.

title: File the Will with Probate Court
description: The original will must be filed with the probate court in the county where the deceased lived. This is required even if you don't plan to open a full probate case. Many states require this by law.
deadline_rule: Typically within 30 days of death — varies by state

title: Petition for Letters Testamentary
description: Letters Testamentary (or Letters of Administration if no will) are court documents that authorize you to act on behalf of the estate. Banks, financial institutions, and government agencies will require these. Your probate attorney will help you file.
deadline_rule: Early in process — needed to access estate accounts

title: Publish Notice to Creditors
description: Most states require you to publish a notice to creditors in a local newspaper and/or notify known creditors by mail. This starts the clock on the creditor claim deadline. Your probate attorney can handle this.
deadline_rule: Shortly after letters testamentary are issued

title: Formally Notify All Beneficiaries
description: All beneficiaries named in the will must be formally notified. This is typically required by law and must be done by certified mail with return receipt. Keep records of all notifications.
deadline_rule: Within 30–60 days of probate opening — varies by state

title: Begin Trust Administration
description: If the deceased had a revocable living trust, it becomes irrevocable at death. The successor trustee takes over immediately without court involvement. Notify financial institutions of the trustee change and follow the trust document's distribution instructions.

title: Notify Banks / Credit Unions
description: Notify each bank where the deceased held accounts. Bring certified death certificates and your letters testamentary. The bank will freeze individual accounts and help you transfer funds to the estate account or directly to beneficiaries if the account had a POD (payable on death) designation.

title: Notify Brokerage / Investment Firms
description: Contact each brokerage where the deceased held accounts. If there are beneficiary designations on file, assets transfer directly. Otherwise, they become part of the probate estate. Gather account statements to inventory all holdings.

title: Process 401(k) / 403(b) Beneficiary Claim
description: Contact the deceased's employer or plan administrator to claim retirement account benefits. If a beneficiary was named, that person claims directly. Distribution rules are complex — consult a tax advisor before taking distributions to avoid large tax bills.

title: IRA Beneficiary Rollover or Distribution
description: Contact the IRA custodian to initiate the beneficiary claim. Spouses can roll the IRA into their own; non-spouse beneficiaries must use an inherited IRA. The SECURE Act 2.0 requires most non-spouse beneficiaries to withdraw within 10 years.

title: Close Credit Card Accounts
description: Notify each credit card issuer of the death. Cancel all accounts and stop autopay arrangements. The estate is responsible for outstanding balances — do not pay from your own funds. Request a final statement for each card.

title: Notify Mortgage Servicer
description: Notify the mortgage servicer of the death. The loan does not automatically disappear — someone must continue payments or the property may face foreclosure. The servicer can explain assumption, refinancing, or sale options. Federal law protects heirs from immediate due-on-sale clauses.

title: Notify Auto Lender
description: Notify the auto lender. Continue making payments to avoid repossession while the estate is being settled. If the vehicle will be transferred to an heir, they can refinance or assume the loan.

title: Cancel Direct Deposits and Recurring Transfers
description: Cancel all direct deposits (pension, Social Security, etc.) and automatic transfers. Any government payments received after death must be returned. Contact each payer individually.
deadline_rule: As soon as possible — overpayments must be returned

title: Access and Inventory Safe Deposit Box
description: Access the safe deposit box with your letters testamentary. Some states require a tax officer present for the initial opening. Document everything with photos or video. Look for the will, deeds, insurance policies, and other important documents.
deadline_rule: Early in process — may contain important documents
documents_needed: Safe deposit box key if available

title: File Life Insurance Death Claim
description: Contact each life insurance company where the deceased had a policy. The named beneficiaries claim directly — these proceeds do not go through probate. Most companies have a claim form. Payment is usually within 30–60 days of a complete claim.
deadline_rule: File promptly — no hard deadline but faster is better

title: Cancel Health Insurance / Notify of COBRA Rights
description: Cancel the deceased's health insurance. If family members were covered under the deceased's employer plan, they have COBRA rights — up to 36 months of continued coverage. Notify the employer's HR department immediately to preserve COBRA eligibility.
contact_phone: Contact the insurer and employer HR

title: Update Auto Insurance Policy
description: Notify the auto insurer. Remove the deceased from the policy if survivors will keep the vehicles, or cancel the policy if all vehicles are being sold. Ensure vehicles remain insured during the estate settlement.

title: Update Homeowner's / Renter's Insurance
description: Notify the insurer and update the policy. An unoccupied home may have reduced coverage under a standard policy — ask about vacancy clauses. Ensure the property remains insured throughout the estate process.
deadline_rule: Within 30 days — vacancy clauses can void coverage

title: File Annuity Death Benefit Claim
description: Contact the annuity company to file a death benefit claim. Annuities with named beneficiaries pass outside of probate. Distribution options include lump sum or continued payments. Tax implications vary — consult a tax advisor.

title: Transfer Real Property Title
description: Real property must be transferred through the probate court or via affidavit (for small estates). A new deed is recorded in the county recorder's office. If the property was held in joint tenancy or in a trust, transfer may happen outside probate.

title: Transfer Vehicle Titles at DMV
description: Transfer vehicle titles to the heirs or sell the vehicles. Requirements vary by state — some allow a simple affidavit for surviving spouses; others require letters testamentary. Contact your state DMV for the exact process.

title: Transfer or Cancel Utilities
description: Transfer utility accounts (electric, gas, water, internet, phone) to the new property owner or cancel them. Keep utilities on if the property is occupied or being maintained. Cancel subscriptions for services no longer needed.

title: Cancel or Transfer Home Security Subscription
description: Contact the home security company to transfer the account to a new owner or cancel service. Early termination fees may apply — ask for a hardship or death waiver.

title: Handle Google Account
description: Use Google's Inactive Account Manager process to request access to or deletion of the deceased's Google account, including Gmail, Google Photos, Drive, and YouTube. Google may require legal documentation and proof of death.

title: Memorialize or Remove Facebook Account
description: Request memorialization (account becomes a memorial page) or removal of the Facebook account. If the deceased designated a Legacy Contact, that person manages the memorial. Submit request through Facebook's Special Request form.
documents_needed: Proof of your relationship to the deceased

title: Handle Apple ID and iCloud
description: If the deceased had an Apple Digital Legacy contact set up, that person can request access to data. Otherwise, Apple requires a court order. Contact Apple Support for guidance. Note: purchases are not transferable.
documents_needed: Access key (if Legacy Contact was set up)

title: Close Amazon Account
description: Contact Amazon to close the account and cancel all subscriptions including Prime. Check for any gift card balances or pending refunds. Amazon does not transfer accounts.

title: Cancel Streaming Subscriptions
description: Cancel all streaming subscriptions (Netflix, Spotify, Hulu, Disney+, Apple TV+, etc.). Check credit card statements to identify all recurring charges. Most can be canceled online through the account settings.

title: Close PayPal / Venmo Account
description: Contact PayPal to close the account and transfer any remaining balance to the estate. Venmo accounts must also be closed. PayPal may require documentation and will mail a check for any remaining balance.

title: Cancel All Recurring Online Subscriptions
description: Review the deceased's credit card and bank statements for the past 3 months to identify all recurring charges. Cancel subscriptions to software, magazines, membership sites, cloud storage, etc. This is often tedious but saves the estate money.

title: File Final Federal Income Tax Return (Form 1040)
description: File a final Form 1040 for the year of death. Write 'DECEASED' and the date of death at the top. A surviving spouse can file jointly. Otherwise, the executor files as 'personal representative'. Include all income earned up to the date of death.
deadline_rule: April 15 of the year following death (or October 15 with extension)

title: File Estate Tax Return (Form 706) if Applicable
description: Form 706 is required if the gross estate exceeds the federal exemption threshold (currently $13.61 million for 2024). Even if not required, it may be wise to file to elect portability of the unused exemption to the surviving spouse.
deadline_rule: 9 months from date of death (or 15 months with extension)
documents_needed: Appraisals for real property and business interests

title: File State Estate Tax Return if Applicable
description: Several states have their own estate tax with lower thresholds than the federal exemption. Check your state's requirements. States with estate taxes include MA, OR, WA, IL, MN, NY, MD, CT, HI, ME, VT, and DC.
deadline_rule: Varies by state — usually 9 months from death

title: Settle Funeral Home Invoice
description: Pay the funeral home invoice from estate funds. Keep receipts — funeral expenses are deductible on the estate tax return (Form 706) if one is filed. If cost is a concern, VA burial benefits can help for veterans.

title: Inventory and Address Outstanding Medical Bills
description: Collect all outstanding medical bills. Submit them to health insurance first — they may cover a portion even after death. Remaining balances become estate debts. Notify creditors that the estate is in probate. You are not personally responsible for these bills.

title: Cancel Prescriptions and Notify Pharmacy
description: Notify the pharmacy to stop all refills. Return unused controlled substances to a pharmacy or DEA take-back location — do not throw them in the trash. Cancel any mail-order prescription services.

title: Notify Primary Care and Specialists
description: Notify all doctors and medical providers of the death. Request copies of medical records if needed for estate purposes or life insurance claims. Cancel all upcoming appointments.
